title: Fix find_member_decl cross-structure name collision
analysis:
The bug: find_member_decl(name) always returns info from the FIRST matching declaration across all structures. When the cursor is inside structure B but A is parsed first, hover shows A's type info for a same-named member. The internal coordination between parsed and compiled lookups (via decl_name) is already present, but the API lacks a way for callers to scope the search to a specific structure.

goto_def.rs already implements the correct pattern: it determines the enclosing declaration by checking if the cursor offset falls within decl.span, then searches only that declaration's members. hover.rs does NOT do this — it calls find_member_decl(word) without any structure scoping.

Additionally, hover.rs line 28 always looks up the evaluated value via templates.first(), which is wrong when the member belongs to a later template.

The fix threads structure name through find_member_decl's API: (1) add a structure parameter to scope both internal lookups, (2) add decl_name to MemberInfo so callers know which structure the member belongs to, (3) add enclosing_structure_name_at helper, (4) update hover.rs to determine the enclosing structure and pass it through.

steps.description: Write failing test find_member_decl_decl_name_populated: verify that MemberInfo includes the owning structure name. Use source with 'structure Foo { param x: Scalar = 1mm }', call find_member_decl("x"), assert info.decl_name == "Foo". This will fail because decl_name field doesn't exist on MemberInfo yet.

Add 'pub decl_name: &'a str' field to MemberInfo. Set it from the decl_name returned by find_parsed_member_span_and_doc in find_member_decl. This is a backward-compatible struct addition (no callers destructure MemberInfo).

Write failing test find_member_decl_scoped_to_second_structure: source has two structures 'structure A { param x: Scalar = 5mm }' and 'structure B { param x: Bool = true }'. Call find_member_decl("x", Some("B")) and assert cell_type is Bool (not Scalar), span is within B's range, and decl_name == "B". This will fail because find_member_decl doesn't accept a structure parameter yet.

Add 'structure: Option<&str>' parameter to find_member_decl. Update find_parsed_member_span_and_doc to accept the same parameter and, when Some, skip declarations whose name doesn't match. Update all existing call sites (hover.rs:16 and ~10 test functions) to pass None, preserving first-match behavior.

Write failing test enclosing_structure_name_at_inside_second: source has two structures A and B. Compute offset of a member inside B, call ctx.enclosing_structure_name_at(offset) and assert it returns Some("B"). Also test offset inside A returns Some("A"), and offset outside any structure returns None. Fails because the method doesn't exist yet.

Add enclosing_structure_name_at(&self, offset: usize) -> Option<&str> method to AnalysisContext. Iterate parsed.declarations, match Structure/Occurrence, check if offset falls within decl.span (same pattern as goto_def.rs lines 25-42), return the decl name.

Write failing hover integration test hover_on_shared_member_in_second_structure: source has 'structure A { param width: Scalar = 5mm }\nstructure B { param width: Bool = true }'. Compute hover at 'width' inside B. Assert markdown contains 'Bool' (not 'Scalar'). This fails because hover.rs still passes None (no scoping).

Update hover.rs compute_hover: (1) call ctx.enclosing_structure_name_at(offset) to get the enclosing structure name, (2) pass it to ctx.find_member_decl(word, enclosing), (3) replace templates.first() value lookup with ctx.get_value(&info.decl_name, word) using the MemberInfo's decl_name field.

design_decisions.decision: Add structure parameter to existing find_member_decl rather than creating a separate find_member_decl_scoped method
design_decisions.rationale: Only two call sites (hover.rs + tests). A single method with Option<&str> is cleaner than two methods. The None behavior preserves the existing first-match semantics for callers that don't have structure context.

design_decisions.decision: Add decl_name field to MemberInfo rather than returning a tuple
design_decisions.rationale: MemberInfo is the natural place for this data — callers (hover.rs) need the owning structure name for the value lookup. Adding a field is backward compatible since no callers destructure the struct.

design_decisions.decision: Follow the enclosing-declaration pattern already in goto_def.rs (lines 25-42)
design_decisions.rationale: goto_def.rs already solves the same problem (scoping member lookup to the enclosing structure). Extracting the pattern into a reusable method on AnalysisContext avoids duplication and establishes a single source of truth.

design_decisions.decision: Fix hover.rs value lookup to use info.decl_name instead of templates.first()
design_decisions.rationale: The templates.first() lookup is a secondary instance of the same cross-structure bug: if the member belongs to structure B but templates[0] is A, the evaluated value comes from the wrong structure. Using decl_name from MemberInfo ensures consistency.

reuse.how: goto_def.rs already iterates parsed declarations and checks offset_u32 >= decl_span.start && offset_u32 < decl_span.end. Extract this into AnalysisContext::enclosing_structure_name_at.
Both StructureDef and OccurrenceDef have SourceSpan fields that cover the full declaration range. Use these for offset containment checks.
Add an optional structure name filter to the existing helper rather than writing a new function. When structure is Some, skip declarations whose name doesn't match.
These tests validate first-match behavior with None scoping. Keep them passing to ensure backward compatibility.
Reuse this helper for the new hover cross-structure integration test.
